content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : OPINION REQUEST MEETING PARTICIPANTS                Date : May 4, 1978


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Alan Herndon

Subject: Definition of a Lobbyist


An employer of lobbyists plans to invite a state legislator
to spend a weekend at a mountain retreat. Accompanying the
official on the trip will be an employee of the lobbyist employer
who is not a lobbyist, but who is familiar with the legislative
concerns of the employer.

ISSUES:

1. Would the time of the employee at the mountain retreat
   be considered time spent influencing legislative or
   administrative action for purposes of regulation 18239?

2. What portion of the employee's time, if any, would be
   considered time spent in direct communication with an
   official for purposes of regulation 18239?

RESOLUTION

1. ALL OF THE TIME SPENT COULD BE CONSIDERED INFLUENCE OR
   ADMINISTRATIVE ACTION FOR PURPOSES OF

2. IN ACCORDANCE WITH A LETTER DATED 6/5/76
   TO MYRON DUBOIS OF FRIENDS OF THE RIVER,
   THE HOURS SPENT IN DIRECT COMMUNICATION ARE
   THOSE HOURS AVAILABLE AND PERSUADE TO COMMUNICATE
                                        A Herndon
                                        6/1/78

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                     Date : May 9, 1978


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Alan Herndon

Subject: Definition of a Lobbyist


On May 8, 1978 the participants of the opinion request meeting

discussed the issues presented in my attached memo of May 4, 1978.

In accordance with advice given to Mark Dubois of the Friends of

the River on June 25, 1976, the issues were resolved as follows:

1.  The entire time spent at the mountain retreat by the

    employee will be time spent "influencing legislative or

    administrative action."

2.  The number of hours available and feasible to communi-

    cate with the official will be time spent in "direct

    communication."

State of California

Memorandum

To : OPINION REQUEST MEETING PARTICIPANTS                    Date : May 4, 1978


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Alan Herndon

Subject: Definition of a Lobbyist


An employer of lobbyists plans to invite a state legislator
to spend a weekend at a mountain retreat. Accompanying the
official on the trip will be an employee of the lobbyist employer
who is not a lobbyist, but who is familiar with the legislative
concerns of the employer.

ISSUES:

1. Would the time of the employee at the mountain retreat
   be considered time spent influencing legislative or
   administrative action for purposes of regulation 18239?

2. What portion of the employee's time, if any, would be
   considered time spent in direct communication with an
   official for purposes of regulation 18239?
